steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: Exemplars of Stigler's law ______ there is the Argand diagram; in astronomy, the Cassegrain reflector; and in physics, the Fermi paradox. The blank must connect a complete independent clause to a list of examples. The correct punctuation mark for that job is a colon, placed immediately after the independent clause ends.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "abound, in mathematics,": A comma after “abound” cannot introduce a list; the additional comma creates a dangling modifier. ✗
  • "abound in mathematics:": Places the colon after “in mathematics,” splitting “in mathematics” away from the rest of the list items and implying exemplars abound only in mathematics. ✗
  • "abound: in mathematics,": Places the colon directly after “abound,” completing the independent clause and then introducing all three list items (“in mathematics,” “in astronomy,” “in physics”) as a parallel series. ✓
  • "abound in mathematics;": A semicolon joins two independent clauses; “in mathematics, there is the Argand diagram” is not an independent clause in context. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
